title: Microsoft Graph Security API - Capability And Routing Overview
applies_to: Routing security-oriented Graph requests to the correct resource family

confirmed_facts.fact: The Microsoft Graph security API provides a unified interface and schema to integrate with security solutions from Microsoft and ecosystem partners, federating queries to onboarded security providers and aggregating responses.

The overview identifies the major Microsoft Graph security areas as advanced hunting, alerts and incidents, attack simulation and training, eDiscovery, audit log query, identity health issues and sensors, identityAccounts, information protection threat assessment, records management, secure score, and threat intelligence.
The newer alerts and incidents API is the recommended alerting surface, while the legacy alerts API is deprecated and scheduled for removal by April 2026.
The advanced hunting APIs in Microsoft Graph replace the older Defender-specific advanced hunting APIs, and the older APIs stop returning data on February 1, 2027.

The overview lists common Microsoft Graph security use cases including list alerts, update alerts, list incidents, list incidents with alerts, update incidents, list eDiscovery cases, list identity health issues, list sensors, list secure scores, and list secure score control profiles.

module_selection_rules: Use /security/alerts_v2 and /security/incidents for SOC-style alert and incident triage.
Use /security/runHuntingQuery when the request is a KQL hunt across Microsoft 365 Defender data instead of entity listing.
Use secureScore and secureScoreControlProfiles for posture and remediation-priority reporting.
Use attackSimulation resources for phishing simulation and training reporting; that capability already exists in a dedicated knowledge entry.
Use threatIntelligence resources only when the tenant has the required Defender Threat Intelligence license and API add-on.
Treat legacy /security/alerts as migration-only unless the user explicitly asks for a deprecated workflow.
Treat identities, eDiscovery, audit, threat assessment, and records management as security-adjacent Graph areas that should be expanded only when a request targets those domains.
implementation_notes: Default to v1.0 security APIs where available. Advanced hunting is available on v1.0 now; do not default to beta for runHuntingQuery unless a required capability is missing from v1.0.

When a user asks to 'work with Graph security', first determine whether they mean detection triage, threat hunting, posture scoring, phishing simulation, threat intelligence, or identity-health workflows.
Build knowledge and code around the microsoft.graph.security namespace for modern security resources, but note that some secure score pages still document the namespace as microsoft.graph.
caveats: Microsoft Graph security is not one flat schema. Alerts, incidents, hunting, secure score, attack simulation, and threat intelligence have distinct permissions, payloads, and licensing constraints.
Several Microsoft Learn security pages require authorization to view full detail, but the surfaced API metadata still establishes routes, permissions, and lifecycle notes.
Some areas in the overview are listed conceptually without full expansion in this pass; deeper extraction should follow the specific resource family when those capabilities are requested.
